altitude_sickness.what_is_it: AMS (Acute Mountain Sickness) occurs when your body doesn't get enough oxygen at high altitude. Almost everyone flying to Leh will experience mild symptoms.
symptoms_severe: Severe headache not relieved by paracetamol
prevention: Fly to Leh and REST for 24-48 hours. Do NOT go sightseeing on Day 1.
Acetazolamide (Diamox) 125mg twice daily starting 1 day before arrival — consult doctor first
treatment: Dexamethasone for severe cases (doctor prescription needed)
Portable hyperbaric bag (Gamow bag) for life-threatening cases
DESCEND if severe — this is the most effective treatment
when_to_evacuate: HACE (High Altitude Cerebral Edema) — confusion, ataxia
HAPE (High Altitude Pulmonary Edema) — breathlessness at rest, pink frothy sputum

medical_facilities.leh_main: SNM Hospital — oxygen, ICU, basic surgery. 01982-252247
medical_facilities.nubra: PHC Diskit — basic OPD only. Serious cases → Leh (5-6 hrs by road)

what_to_carry: First aid kit with bandages, antiseptic

medical_facilities.kaza: CHC Kaza — very basic. Referral to Kullu/Manali (8-10 hrs road).

key_advice: Extremely remote. Stock up on medicines before entering Spiti.
Road access only from Manali or Shimla — both 8-10+ hours.
No ATMs in Spiti valley. Carry cash.
Mobile network: only BSNL works, and barely.
For emergencies: helicopter evacuation through 108/disaster management.

medical_facilities.north_sikkim: Very limited. Plan evacuation to Gangtok.

key_advice: Inner Line Permit required for North Sikkim — arrange through travel agent
Gurudongmar Lake at 5,430m — spend time acclimatizing in Lachung/Lachen first
AMS risk is real. Don't rush to high altitude.
Carry altitude sickness medicine and oxygen canister.
STNM Gangtok is 4-5 hours from North Sikkim.
